name: current-2026-learned-championship-tail
description: Focused 2026 replay testing whether a no-future learned championship tail residual correction can replace hand-shaped phase/residual boosts.
modelNames: No-Future Residual-Ridge Scaled-Archetype Ensemble K=1.10 W=0.20 P=0.00 S=0.15 M=8 CR=0.100 DR=0.050 RW=0.25 L=30 C=40
No-Future Champs-PhaseResidualBoost Residual-Ridge Scaled-Archetype Ensemble K=1.10 W=0.20 P=0.00 S=0.15 M=8 CR=0.100 DR=0.050 RW=0.25 L=30 C=40 E=4 M=6 RB=0.15/0.25/0 N=8 W=18 POS
No-Future LearnedTail Residual-Ridge Scaled-Archetype Ensemble K=1.10 W=0.20 P=0.00 S=0.15 M=8 CR=0.100 DR=0.050 RW=0.25 L=30 C=40 TW=0.35 TL=25 TN=24 TC=30 F=PRS POS
No-Future LearnedTail Residual-Ridge Scaled-Archetype Ensemble K=1.10 W=0.20 P=0.00 S=0.15 M=8 CR=0.100 DR=0.050 RW=0.25 L=30 C=40 TW=0.50 TL=15 TN=16 TC=35 F=PRS POS
No-Future LearnedTail Residual-Ridge Scaled-Archetype Ensemble K=1.10 W=0.20 P=0.00 S=0.15 M=8 CR=0.100 DR=0.050 RW=0.25 L=30 C=40 TW=0.35 TL=40 TN=24 TC=25 F=PR
notes: LearnedTail fits only from prior residuals in championship-like contexts.
F=PRS uses phase, event residual, and score-scale features; F=PR drops score-scale features.
The conservative residual baseline remains the defended default unless the learned tail improves score without spending margin/Brier.
